# This solution uses Dynamic Programming optimized with Segment Trees to achieve O(N log M) complexity, where N is the length of nums and M is the maximum value in nums.

def solve(nums, k)
    n = nums.length
    if n == 0
        return 0
    end
    
    V_MAX = 100000
    
    # Helper class for Segment Tree supporting Range Maximum Query and Point Update (Max)
    class SegmentTree
        def initialize(size)
            # We use 1-based indexing for values [1, size]
            @size = size
            # Tree array size: 4*size is safe
            @tree = Array.new(4 * size + 1, -1) # Initialize with a value lower than any possible score (scores are non-negative)
        end
        
        # Point update: updates the value at 'idx' to max(current_value, new_val)
        # idx is 1-based value index
        def update(idx, new_val)
            update_recursive(1, 1, @size, idx, new_val)
        end
        
        def update_recursive(node, start, end, idx, new_val)
            if start == end
                @tree[node] = [@tree[node], new_val].max
                return
            end
            
            mid = (start + end) / 2
            
            if idx <= mid
                update_recursive(2 * node, start, mid, idx, new_val)
            else
                update_recursive(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, idx, new_val)
            end
            
            # Update current node: max of children
            @tree[node] = [@tree[2 * node], @tree[2 * node + 1]].max
        end
        
        # Range Maximum Query for range [L, R] (inclusive, 1-based value indices)
        def query(l, r)
            return -1 if l > r
            query_recursive(1, 1, @size, l, r)
        end
        
        def query_recursive(node, start, end, l, r)
            # Range represented by node is completely outside [l, r]
            if r < start || end < l
                return -1 # Sentinel value for max query
            end
            
            # Range represented by node is completely inside [l, r]
            if l <= start && end <= r
                return @tree[node]
            end
            
            # Overlap
            mid = (start + end) / 2
            p1 = query_recursive(2 * node, start, mid, l, r)
            p2 = query_recursive(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, l, r)
            
            [p1, p2].max
        end
    end
    
    # DP_UP[i]: Max score ending at i, where nums[i] > nums[j]
    # DP_DOWN[i]: Max score ending at i, where nums[i] < nums[j]
    dp_up = Array.new(n, 0)
    dp_down = Array.new(n, 0)
    
    # ST_UP stores DP_UP[j] indexed by nums[j]
    st_up = SegmentTree.new(V_MAX)
    # ST_DOWN stores DP_DOWN[j] indexed by nums[j]
    st_down = SegmentTree.new(V_MAX)
    
    max_score = 0
    
    for i in 0...n
        # 1. Check if index j = i - k is eligible to update the STs
        j = i - k
        if j >= 0
            # Update STs with scores calculated at j
            # We only update if dp_up[j] or dp_down[j] are positive, though the structure handles -1 initialization
            if dp_up[j] > -1
                st_up.update(nums[j], dp_up[j])
            end
            if dp_down[j] > -1
                st_down.update(nums[j], dp_down[j])
            end
        end
        
        # 2. Calculate DP_UP[i] (Requires nums[j] < nums[i], so j is in range [1, nums[i] - 1])
        # Max previous DP_DOWN score among eligible j
        max_prev_down = st_down.query(1, nums[i] - 1)
        
        # Length 1 sequence score is nums[i]. If max_prev_down is -1, we take 0.
        prev_score_up = [0, max_prev_down].max
        dp_up[i] = nums[i] + prev_score_up
        
        # 3. Calculate DP_DOWN[i] (Requires nums[j] > nums[i], so j is in range [nums[i] + 1, V_MAX])
        # Max previous DP_UP score among eligible j
        max_prev_up = st_up.query(nums[i] + 1, V_MAX)
        
        # Length 1 sequence score is nums[i]. If max_prev_up is -1, we take 0.
        prev_score_down = [0, max_prev_up].max
        dp_down[i] = nums[i] + prev_score_down
        
        # Update overall maximum score
        max_score = [max_score, dp_up[i]].max
        max_score = [max_score, dp_down[i]].max
    end
    
    return max_score
end

class SegmentTree
    def initialize(size)
        # Size corresponds to V_max + 1. We use 1-based indexing for values 1 to size-1.
        @size = size
        # The tree array needs sufficient size, 4 * size is safe.
        @tree = Array.new(4 * size) { 0 } 
    end

    # Updates the value at index idx (representing the value nums[i])
    # We store the maximum score seen so far for this value.
    def update(idx, val)
        update_recursive(1, 1, @size - 1, idx, val)
    end

    # Performs Range Maximum Query for range [l, r]
    def query(l, r)
        return 0 if l > r
        query_recursive(1, 1, @size - 1, l, r)
    end

    private

    def update_recursive(node, start, end, idx, val)
        if start == end
            @tree[node] = [@tree[node], val].max
            return
        end

        mid = (start + end) / 2
        if idx <= mid
            update_recursive(2 * node, start, mid, idx, val)
        else
            update_recursive(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, idx, val)
        end
        
        @tree[node] = [@tree[2 * node], @tree[2 * node + 1]].max
    end

    def query_recursive(node, start, end, l, r)
        # No overlap
        if r < start || end < l
            return 0
        end

        # Complete overlap
        if l <= start && end <= r
            return @tree[node]
        end

        # Partial overlap
        mid = (start + end) / 2
        p1 = query_recursive(2 * node, start, mid, l, r)
        p2 = query_recursive(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, l, r)
        
        [p1, p2].max
    end

        current_val = nums[i]

# Helper structure for Segment Tree (Range Maximum Query)
# Since we are dealing with maximum values, the internal array stores max values.
class SegmentTree
    def initialize(size)
        # Size is the maximum value index (V_max)
        @size = size
        # Tree array size is typically 4 * size
        @tree = Array.new(4 * size + 1, 0)
    end

    # Point update: Update the maximum value at a specific index.
    # index is 1-based value (1 to @size)
    def update(node, start, end, index, val)
        if start == end
            # Leaf node: update max
            @tree[node] = [@tree[node], val].max
            return
        end

        mid = (start + end) / 2
        if index <= mid
            # Go left
            update(2 * node, start, mid, index, val)
        else
            # Go right
            update(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, index, val)
        end

        # Internal node: update max from children
        @tree[node] = [@tree[2 * node], @tree[2 * node + 1]].max
    end

    # Public update interface
    def point_update(index, val)
        update(1, 1, @size, index, val)
    end

    # Range maximum query: Find max in range [L, R] (1-based indices)
    def query(node, start, end, L, R)
        # Current segment is outside query range
        if R < start || end < L
            return 0 # Identity for max operation
        end

        # Current segment is completely inside query range
        if L <= start && end <= R
            return @tree[node]
        end

        # Partial overlap
        mid = (start + end) / 2
        
        p1 = query(2 * node, start, mid, L, R)
        p2 = query(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, L, R)
        
        return [p1, p2].max
    end

    # Public query interface
    def range_query(L, R)
        if L > R || L < 1 || R > @size
            return 0
        end
        query(1, 1, @size, L, R)
    end
end


def solve(nums, k)
    n = nums.length
    if n == 0
        return 0
    end

    V_max = 100000 # Maximum possible value of an element in nums

    # DP[i][0]: Max score ending at i, where nums[i] is a peak (prev < current)
    # DP[i][1]: Max score ending at i, where nums[i] is a valley (prev > current)
    # Initialize with 0.
    dp = Array.new(n) { [0, 0] }

    # Segment Trees indexed by value (1 to V_max)
    # ST_0 tracks max DP[j][0] values for available j's
    st0 = SegmentTree.new(V_max)
    # ST_1 tracks max DP[j][1] values for available j's
    st1 = SegmentTree.new(V_max)

    max_score = 0

    for i in 0...n do
        v = nums[i]
        
        # Phase 1: Inject ready updates from index j = i - k
        j = i - k
        if j >= 0
            # DP[j] must have been calculated in a previous iteration (j < i).
            # Inject DP[j] into the segment trees for use in calculating DP[i] and future DP[i']
            st0.point_update(nums[j], dp[j][0])
            st1.point_update(nums[j], dp[j][1])
        end

        # Phase 2: Calculate DP[i][0] (Ends in <: must follow a sequence ending in > (State 1))
        # Need max DP[j][1] where nums[j] < v (i.e., query range [1, v-1])
        m1 = st1.range_query(1, v - 1)
        # Length 1 sequence (score v) is implicitly covered by max(0, m1) if m1=0
        dp[i][0] = v + [0, m1].max

        # Phase 2: Calculate DP[i][1] (Ends in >: must follow a sequence ending in < (State 0))
        # Need max DP[j][0] where nums[j] > v (i.e., query range [v+1, V_max])
        m0 = st0.range_query(v + 1, V_max)
        dp[i][1] = v + [0, m0].max

        # Update overall maximum score
        max_score = [max_score, dp[i][0], dp[i][1]].max
    end

    return max_score
end

# Helper structure for Segment Tree (Range Maximum Query)
# Since we are dealing with maximum values, the internal array stores max values.
class SegmentTree
    def initialize(size)
        # Size is the maximum value index (V_max)
        @size = size
        # Tree array size is typically 4 * size
        @tree = Array.new(4 * size + 1, 0)
    end

    # Point update: Update the maximum value at a specific index.
    # index is 1-based value (1 to @size)
    def update(node, start, end, index, val)
        if start == end
            # Leaf node: update max
            @tree[node] = [@tree[node], val].max
            return
        end

        mid = (start + end) / 2
        if index <= mid
            # Go left
            update(2 * node, start, mid, index, val)
        else
            # Go right
            update(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, index, val)
        end

        # Internal node: update max from children
        @tree[node] = [@tree[2 * node], @tree[2 * node + 1]].max
    end

    # Public update interface
    def point_update(index, val)
        update(1, 1, @size, index, val)
    end

    # Range maximum query: Find max in range [L, R] (1-based indices)
    def query(node, start, end, L, R)
        # Current segment is outside query range
        if R < start || end < L
            return 0 # Identity for max operation
        end

        # Current segment is completely inside query range
        if L <= start && end <= R
            return @tree[node]
        end

        # Partial overlap
        mid = (start + end) / 2
        
        p1 = query(2 * node, start, mid, L, R)
        p2 = query(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, L, R)
        
        return [p1, p2].max
    end

    # Public query interface
    def range_query(L, R)
        if L > R || L < 1 || R > @size
            return 0
        end
        query(1, 1, @size, L, R)
    end
end


def solve(nums, k)
    n = nums.length
    if n == 0
        return 0
    end

    V_max = 100000 # Maximum possible value of an element in nums

    # DP[i][0]: Max score ending at i, where nums[i] is a peak (prev < current)
    # DP[i][1]: Max score ending at i, where nums[i] is a valley (prev > current)
    # Initialize with 0.
    dp = Array.new(n) { [0, 0] }

    # Segment Trees indexed by value (1 to V_max)
    # ST_0 tracks max DP[j][0] values for available j's
    st0 = SegmentTree.new(V_max)
    # ST_1 tracks max DP[j][1] values for available j's
    st1 = SegmentTree.new(V_max)

    max_score = 0

    for i in 0...n do
        v = nums[i]
        
        # Phase 1: Inject ready updates from index j = i - k
        j = i - k
        if j >= 0
            # DP[j] must have been calculated in a previous iteration (j < i).
            # Inject DP[j] into the segment trees for use in calculating DP[i] and future DP[i']
            st0.point_update(nums[j], dp[j][0])
            st1.point_update(nums[j], dp[j][1])
        end

        # Phase 2: Calculate DP[i][0] (Ends in <: must follow a sequence ending in > (State 1))
        # Need max DP[j][1] where nums[j] < v (i.e., query range [1, v-1])
        m1 = st1.range_query(1, v - 1)
        # Length 1 sequence (score v) is implicitly covered by max(0, m1) if m1=0
        dp[i][0] = v + [0, m1].max

        # Phase 2: Calculate DP[i][1] (Ends in >: must follow a sequence ending in < (State 0))
        # Need max DP[j][0] where nums[j] > v (i.e., query range [v+1, V_max])
        m0 = st0.range_query(v + 1, V_max)
        dp[i][1] = v + [0, m0].max

        # Update overall maximum score
        max_score = [max_score, dp[i][0], dp[i][1]].max
    end

    return max_score
end
